---
title: "Scheduling Worksheet"
format: html
---

## Scheduling Worksheet

::: callout
### Instructions

This worksheet is designed to help you plan and track your day. Use it to build structure, balance responsibilities, and make time for recovery activities.

1.  Start with sleep. Write down the hours you plan to sleep (from \_\_\_ to \_\_\_). Make sure you are getting enough rest.

2.  Fill in each hour. Write what you plan to do during each time block (work, appointments, meals, exercise, recovery activities, relaxation, etc.).

3.  Be realistic. Don’t overload your schedule. Include time for self-care and breaks.

4.  Use the Notes section. Add anything you need to remember about the day, such as special tasks or reflections.

5.  Use the Reminders section. Write important things you want to carry with you (affirmations, goals, triggers to avoid, support contacts).
:::

### Sleep Planning

How many hours will you sleep? \_\_\_\_\_\_\_

From \_\_\_\_\_\_\_ To \_\_\_\_\_\_\_

| Time     | Activity / Plan |
|----------|-----------------|
| 7:00 AM  |                 |
| 8:00 AM  |                 |
| 9:00 AM  |                 |
| 10:00 AM |                 |
| 11:00 AM |                 |
| 12:00 PM |                 |
| 1:00 PM  |                 |
| 2:00 PM  |                 |
| 3:00 PM  |                 |
| 4:00 PM  |                 |
| 5:00 PM  |                 |
| 6:00 PM  |                 |
| 7:00 PM  |                 |
| 8:00 PM  |                 |
| 9:00 PM  |                 |
| 10:00 PM |                 |
| 11:00 PM |                 |

### Notes:

### Reminders:
